reasoning: The 10-year TIPS yield (DFII10) generally follows a random walk, making long-term forecasting difficult and pushing probabilities close to 0.5. The FRED statistical tool indicated a slight short-term probability of an increase (0.58) based on a mild upward trend over the last 30 days. However, recent momentum has flattened, with the last two recorded days both sitting at 2.08. Expecting a near random walk going forward, the probability of the yield being strictly higher than 2.08 will start slightly above 0.50 due to the short-term trend, and then steadily regress towards exactly 0.50 over longer horizons, correctly increasing uncertainty for further out dates (May 18 -> Nov 7).
